content:
- Closeout complete. Ran task_switch.sh, committed the task result, and pushed to origin/master.
- Final product fix included the HA ADT cleanup plus the follow-on regression fixes: replica upstream recovery from primary_conninfo and system_identifier-based basebackup_required classification.
- Did not touch unrelated local change in .agents/skills/ralph-log/SKILL.md; worktree is otherwise clean for task-scoped files.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md to choose the next task under the bug-first and blocker rules.
- Resolved blockers: cert-reload is unblocked because story-ctl task 08 passes true; config-simplification remains blocked because story-ctl-operator-experience still has active task 09; runtime/node shrink is unblocked because story-ctl task 10 passes true; postgres-role-reconciliation remains blocked behind config-simplification.
- No failing bug tasks are active. Among unblocked failing non-bug tasks, the only high-priority task is .ralph/tasks/story-general-architecture-improvement-finding/03-task-refactor-the-ha-process-boundary-around-a-dedicated-process-intent-adapter-and-remove-secret-bearing-process-defaults-from-ha.md, so that is the correct next selection. Next step is to write that exact path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately.

- Started new turn by reading the prior progress log and current task pointer.
- Active task is story-general-architecture-improvement-finding/03-task-refactor-the-ha-process-boundary-around-a-dedicated-process-intent-adapter-and-remove-secret-bearing-process-defaults-from-ha.md.
- Reading the task body now to determine whether this turn is still TO BE VERIFIED type-design work or NOW EXECUTE implementation work.

- Task file is still in design-first mode; there was no NOW EXECUTE marker.
- Switched into boundary-inspection mode and started reading the HA/process files and API exposure points.
- Intention for this turn: reshape types and ownership first, tolerate temporary compile breakage, then update the task file to NOW EXECUTE and stop.

- Confirmed the main design seam: process should own an explicit ProcessIntent ADT plus the secret-bearing translation context, while HA should only carry typed intent and non-process side effects.
- Also confirmed that HaState is currently serialized directly in the API, so planned commands need a dedicated public read-model type instead of leaking internal execution enums.
- Starting the broad type-definition sweep now; compile breakage is acceptable in this phase because the task is still pre-NOW-EXECUTE.

- Inspected the HA/process boundary and confirmed the main ownership leaks: HaWorkerCtx carries ProcessDispatchDefaults with auth/SSL/remote source data; HA builds ProcessJobRequest and concrete specs directly; HaState exposes planned_commands as the mixed internal ReconcileAction list.
- Decided on the type redesign for this pass: replace ReconcileAction with a structured ReconcilePlan, move the HA->process channel to process-owned ProcessIntentRequest, enrich process-owned ActiveJobKind so start mode is not lossy, and remove ProcessDispatchDefaults from HA entirely.
- About to patch types across ha/state.rs, ha/types.rs, ha/reconcile.rs, ha/process_dispatch.rs, ha/worker.rs, process/jobs.rs, process/state.rs, process/mod.rs, runtime/node.rs, and the task file. This pass will intentionally leave compile errors for the later NOW EXECUTE phase.

- Completed the broad type sweep in the main boundary files: HA state no longer carries process defaults, HA now plans deliberate PlannedActionView values, and the process domain now defines the authoritative ProcessIntent ADTs and process-owned materialization context.
- Removed HA-owned source/auth assembly by deleting ha/source_conn.rs and adding process/source.rs as the new process-owned source materialization home.
- I am only doing small consistency cleanups now before writing the execution plan and NOW EXECUTE marker back into the task file.

- Finished the pre-execution type-design pass for the HA/process boundary task.
- Main design outcome: HA now points toward a `ReconcilePlan`/`PlannedActions` shape, process owns the new `ProcessIntent` ADTs plus process-owned source/materialization config, and HA no longer carries the old ProcessDispatchDefaults bag in its state types.
- Deleted `src/ha/source_conn.rs`, added `src/process/source.rs`, rewired `src/ha/state.rs`, `src/ha/process_dispatch.rs`, `src/process/jobs.rs`, `src/process/state.rs`, `src/runtime/node.rs`, `src/ha/reconcile.rs`, `src/ha/worker.rs`, and `src/ha/types.rs` to the new type direction. Tree is intentionally mid-refactor and not compile-clean yet.
- Appended the execution plan to the task file and ended it with NOW EXECUTE.
- Next turn must execute the plan: finish reconciling worker/process implementation against the new ADTs, make it compile, then run make check/lint/test/test-long and docs only after all tests pass.

- Started the execution turn for the active HA/process boundary task.
- Prior progress shows the task file already ended with NOW EXECUTE, so this turn will finish the implementation, recover compile/test health, and only do docs after all checks pass.
- Reading the task body and current git status now before fixing the refactor fallout.

- Inspected the current HA/process boundary refactor without making edits, focused on stale references to ReconcileAction, ProcessJobRequest, ProcessJobKind, and ShutdownMode plus old planned_commands read-model assumptions.
- Confirmed the real runtime/code leftovers are concentrated in src/process/worker.rs (still typed against old ProcessJobRequest/ProcessJobKind) and in src/ha/reconcile.rs tests + src/ha/types.rs compatibility code for old ReconcileAction.
- Confirmed there are no remaining Rust API/controller/read-model code paths expecting planned_commands; HaState already exposes planned_actions. The remaining old planned-command assumptions are docs source under docs/src plus generated docs/book artifacts.
- Next if continuing from this report: update process worker/tests from ProcessJob* to ProcessExecution*/intent pipeline, delete ReconcileAction compatibility/test leftovers, and refresh docs from planned_commands/ReconcileAction to planned_actions/PlannedActions or the intended public read-model wording.

- Confirmed the main execution blockers: ha/types still needed the explicit ReconcilePlan type, reconcile tests still asserted old ReconcileAction values, and process/worker plus logging real tests still referenced ProcessJobRequest/ProcessJobKind.
- Patched the HA side to introduce a separate ReconcilePlan struct and started rewiring reconcile/worker to use it while keeping HaState on the PlannedActions read model.
- Next focus is the process worker: materialize ProcessIntentRequest into ProcessExecutionRequest inside process/worker.rs, then update affected tests and rerun make check.

- All required validation gates are now green: make check, make lint, make test, and make test-long all passed after fixing the process-intent migration and the logging test helper watch-subscriber lifetime.
- Switching to the k2-docs-loop skill now because docs updates are only allowed after tests pass and the task explicitly requires that skill for docs work.
- Next steps are to update stale planned_commands/ReconcileAction documentation to the new planned_actions/process-intent boundary, regenerate docs artifacts if needed, then perform task closeout.

- Docs source pages were updated after the full gate run using the k2-docs-loop workflow as the required docs path, then rebuilt with make docs-build and checked with make docs-lint.
- Updated docs now refer to ha.planned_actions and the structured process-intent boundary instead of the old planned_commands/ReconcileAction model.
- About to do Ralph closeout: task file already marked completed/passing, next is task_switch.sh, git add/commit/push, and then final response.
